calendar.matters:
(Amended) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal
Off Calendar Dec-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KUTE (Amended) Motion for Order to Set Aside and Vacate Dismissal. Appearances: Russell J. Callison, Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415) 4438-6622 appearing for Defendant Akay and Associates and opposing party; Michael Kute, (415) 879-1268, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and moving party; Ray Z. Bacerdo, Esq. (510) 287-4000 Attorney at Law, appearing for Defendant Issa Michael and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Plaintiff fails to show surprise, mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. (CCP section 473.) Request for attorney fees denied without prejudice to filing an attorney fees motion. (Copy of Reply with attachments is returned to Plaintiff at the hearing.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Tamra Keen CSR # 5404

Notice Of Motion For Order To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL KUTE Motion For Order To Set Aside And Vacate Dismissal. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to December 17, 2009 per amended notice of motion. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Response To Deft'S Discovery; And For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctins Against Pltf
Off Calendar Apr-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendants Issa Michael's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
a. All objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within seven days of the date of service of this order, plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendants' form interrogatories (set one);
c. As to defendants' amended request for admissions:
i. Requests for admission nos. 1 through 7, inclusive; 13 and 14; motion granted; the specified requests are deemed admitted;
ii. As to the matters set forth in requests for admission nos. 8 through 12, inclusive, motion denied;
2. Request for monetary sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $390.00, payable within 30 days of the date of service of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Set For Trial On Oct-26-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Oct-07-09 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 602

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jonathan Rizzardi, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Douglas N. Akay

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
1. Defendant Douglas N. Akay's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
a. All objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within five days of the date of service of this order, plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendants' form interrogatories and request for production of documents (set one);
c. As to defendants' request for admissions:
i. Requests for admission nos. 1 through 27, inclusive; motion granted; the specified requests are deemed admitted;
ii. As to the matters set forth in requests for admission nos. 20 motion denied;

2. Request for monetary sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $390.00, payable within 30 days of the date of service of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
